propositions: The code-review gate cast zero BLOCK votes across 162 changes despite confirmed must-fix findings, because the 0.95 default threshold is unreachable under an observed impact ceiling of 0.60.

Derive the empirically-correct block threshold in priority units (validity × impact) by computing precision(block-worthy | priority >= t) over candidate thresholds and selecting the smallest t where precision > 0.5, tie-broken toward fewer false blocks.
Assemble and commit an adjudicated code-v2 finding corpus projected straight from stored sidecar validity/impact/priority fields, labeling each finding block-worthy, not-block-worthy, or ambiguous.
Use a dedicated derive_code_review_threshold.py reading code_review_result_v1 sidecars rather than the plan-review calibrator, which cannot consume the code-review schema.
Run the ADR-0036 A/B impact-model separation gate to confirm the code-v2 model separates HIGH from NIT before deriving the threshold from the real corpus.
Commit the threshold recommendation as a CI-verifiable repo artifact under docs/experiments/ and cover the precision-over-priority logic with a test under tests/.
